title: Here’s what experts are predicting the Patriots’ 2026 record will be
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Experts predict a 10-7 record for the Patriots in 2026, a decline from their 14-3 season last year.
The Patriots face a tough schedule, including teams like Seattle, Denver, and Chicago, impacting their expected win total.
Various analysts agree on a 10-7 prediction, except Sports Illustrated, predicting 9-8, and Pats Pulpit with a 12-5 outlook.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: "Marty, Life is Short," a Martin Short documentary, is now streaming on Netflix, directed by Lawrence Kasdan.
"Mars Attacks!" is available for free streaming on YouTube and Plex, revisiting Tim Burton's 1996 sci-fi comedy.
"Pop Culture Jeopardy!" Season 2, hosted by Colin Jost, has moved to Netflix, featuring duos competing in pop culture knowledge.

title: Mayor Wu remains firm as councilors consider rejecting her budget
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Mayor Wu's $4.9 billion budget proposal for Boston faces potential rejection by City Council members seeking increased spending.
Councilors have until June 10 to decide on accepting, rejecting, or amending the mayor's budget proposal.
Wu warns that rejecting her budget could lead to further spending cuts due to statewide financial developments.

title: Scholars bar is closing after 15 years. A new concept is already planned.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Scholars American Bistro & Cocktail Lounge in Downtown Crossing is closing after 15 years, with its last service this Saturday.
The location at 25 School St. will reopen in late summer as Off The Rails Boston, a live music-focused restaurant and bar.
The new concept will offer bands, live programming, and Americana-inspired food, influenced by Nashville and Boston's culture.

title: How much are you spending to attend the World Cup?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston hosts seven FIFA World Cup 2026 matches at the temporarily renamed Boston Stadium starting June 13.
Commuter Rail tickets from South Station to Foxborough during match days cost $80 round-trip per person.
Hotel prices have surged around Greater Boston, with parking near the stadium costing about $175 on match days.

title: A first look at the massive outdoor bar coming to Cape Cod this summer
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Backyard by Foley's, a 33,650 sq ft outdoor bar, will open in Dennis Port, Cape Cod this summer.
The venue will feature a 5,000 sq ft sail-covered bar, picnic areas, live music, and kids' activity zones.
Collaborations include rotating food trucks, pop-up ice cream, farmers markets, and outdoor events at 645 Main Street.

title: Chelsea man pleads not guilty after allegedly threatening BU campus, students, deans
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Maximillien Sajous, 29, of Chelsea, pleaded not guilty to charges threatening Boston University campus and personnel.
Sajous faces felony stalking and bomb threat charges, plus misdemeanor harassment and threat charges, court records show.
He is held without bail as dangerous; his enrollment at BU and summer internship were terminated.

title: Healey refuses to alter RMV policy that denies undercover license plates to ICE
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey refuses to change RMV policy denying undercover plates to ICE vehicles in Massachusetts.
The Trump administration threatens legal action, claiming RMV policy is unconstitutional and endangers ICE agents.
RMV limits confidential plates to active criminal investigations, excluding most ICE civil enforcement actions.

title: Body of Quincy kayaker pulled from Boston Harbor
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Tri Trinh, 72, was found dead in Boston Harbor near Thompson Island after kayaking from a Quincy beach.
Authorities reported no obvious signs of trauma or foul play in Trinh's death; autopsy results are pending.
Trinh's body was transported to Beth Israel Deaconess Hospital — Milton following recovery from the harbor.

title: South Shore school employee pleads guilty to stealing food, equipment for ‘Snack Shack’
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Patrick Van Cott, 64, pleaded guilty to stealing food and equipment from Plymouth Public Schools to support his 'Snack Shack'.
Van Cott used school and USDA funds to purchase thousands of dollars in supplies, including refrigerators and burger patties.
Van Cott faces up to 30 years in prison and $500,000 in fines for theft and wire fraud charges.
